mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS declines, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.0% signals severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which directly boosts corporate revenues and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive returns with >55% accuracy, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals immediate supply-side contraction that triggers short-term equity sell-offs, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of triggered instances, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets price in inflation without selling off, yielding positive 20-day returns in >65% of high-inflation months, or the signal shows zero incremental predictive value over baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion that increases corporate profit expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are negative in >50% of quarters following positive GDPC1 growth, or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.05 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing federal funds rate increases signals supply-side resilience that absorbs monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets decline in >60% of periods when supply growth outpaces rate hikes, or the mechanism fails to maintain a positive Sharpe ratio in out-of-sample testing.
